result.stdout:
2026-03-28 04:10:44.923 +00:00 [Information] 1: fsi started
Description:

Usage:
  fsi [options]

Options:
  -f, --fhir-version <R3|R4>                                            FHIR version of the input
  --sourceType <Filesystem|MongoDb|None>                                Kind of source FSI will use to import resources
  -s, --source <source>                                                 Input directory for work (this directory is visited recursively including all the subdirectories)
  -l, --limit <limit>                                                   Limit the number of resources to import. Use this for testing your setup.
  --update-existing-resources <ErrorOnConflict|False|OnlyIfNewer|True>  When true, a resource is updated in the database if it already exists and a history record is created. Otherwise, an existing resource gets skipped.
  --license <license>                                                   Firely Server license file
  --dbType <MongoDb|MongoV2|PubSub|SQL>                                 Specifies the target database type
  --settings <settings>                                                 Specifies the path of a custom settings json file
  --readBuffer <readBuffer>                                             Number of resources to buffer after reading.
  --metaPar <metaPar>                                                   Number of threads to assign metadata.
  --metaBuffer <metaBuffer>                                             Number of resources to buffer for assigning metadata.
  --typePar <typePar>                                                   Number of threads to add type information.
  --typeBuffer <typeBuffer>                                             Number of resources to buffer for adding type information.
  --absRelPar <absRelPar>                                               Number of threads when converting absolute to relative references.
  --absRelBuffer <absRelBuffer>                                         Number of resources to buffer when converting absolute to relative references.
  --indexPar <indexPar>                                                 Number of threads to index the search parameters. This is typically the most resource intensive step and should have the most threads.
  --indexBuffer <indexBuffer>                                           Number of resources to buffer for indexing the search parameters.
  --haltOnError                                                         When true, stop application on single error.
  --useRecoveryJournal <useRecoveryJournal>                             A directory containing the recovery journal.
  --urlConvBases <urlConvBases>                                         Base endpoints for which conversion should be performed.
  --urlConvElems <urlConvElems>                                         FhirPaths identifying URLs elements that should be converted from absolute to relative URLs.
  --extraParameters <extraParameters>                                   Internal - side-load additional search parameters from a json file
  --provisionTargetDatabase                                             Provision the target database
  --mongoCollection <mongoCollection>                                   Collection name for entries
  --mongoConnectionstring <mongoConnectionstring>                       Connection string to Firely Server MongoDb database
  --mongoPar <mongoPar>                                                 The # of batches to save in parallel. Depends on your bandwidth to MongoDb and its processing power.
  --mongoExistQryPar <mongoExistQryPar>                                 The number of parallel threads querying the DB to check whether a resource exists (only when --update-existing-resources is set to false).
  --mongoBatch <mongoBatch>                                             The # of resources to save in each batch.
  -c, --connectionstring <connectionstring>                             Connection string to Firely Server SQL Server database
  --sqlPar <sqlPar>                                                     The # of batches to save in parallel. Depends on your bandwidth to SQL Server and its processing power.
  --sqlExistQryPar <sqlExistQryPar>                                     The number of parallel threads querying the DB to check whether a resource exists (only when --update-existing-resources is set to false).
  --sqlBatch <sqlBatch>                                                 The # of resources to save in each batch. SQL Server must be able to process it within the CommandTimeout.
  --sqlTimeout <sqlTimeout>                                             The time SQL Server is allowed to process a batch of resources.
  --mongoV2ConnectionResources <mongoV2ConnectionResources>             Connection string to Firely Server MongoDb database for resources
  --mongoV2ConnectionFhirIndex <mongoV2ConnectionFhirIndex>             Connection string to Firely Server MongoDb database for the search index
  --mongoV2Par <mongoV2Par>                                             The # of batches to save in parallel. Depends on your bandwidth to MongoDb and its processing power.
  --mongoV2ExistQryPar <mongoV2ExistQryPar>                             The number of parallel threads querying the DB to check whether a resource exists (only when --update-existing-resources is set to false).
  --mongoV2Batch <mongoV2Batch>                                         The # of resources to save in each batch.
  --srcMongoConnectionString <srcMongoConnectionString>                 Connection string to read resources from
  --srcMongoCollection <srcMongoCollection>                             Collection name to read entries from
  --srcMongoRunningMode <AdHoc|Continuous>                              The mode in which application should run
  --version                                                             Show version information
  -?, -h, --help                                                        Show help and usage information


2026-03-28 04:10:45.087 +00:00 [Information] 1: fsi completed with exitCode 0 : Success
